metadata.label: extra/Ramp-Platform
metadata.title: Software Engineer, Infrastructure
metadata.team_direction: Ramp is building the smart infrastructure for finance teams, automating how over $100B in annualized spend flows in and out of 50,000+ companies

official_notes: No recovered historical Claude-vs-official target baseline; used only for old-pipe vs new-pipe Codex comparison.

findings.issue: Body copy bolds qualifiers and limiting phrases such as **team-maintained**, **existing**, and **internal**. This violates the styling rule and dilutes the visual emphasis meant for technologies and quantified scope.
findings.fix: Remove bold from modifiers and keep bolding for technologies, system nouns, and quantified outcomes only.

findings.field: Professional Summary, Skills, and cloud-related bullets across TikTok and DiDi
findings.issue: The resume presents AWS, Azure, and GCP as simultaneously core, plus Docker/Kubernetes/Terraform, which reads like same-category stack breadth without enough weighting. This is a stack-stuffing risk even though the individual mentions are supported.
findings.fix: Anchor each cloud platform to the specific role that used it and reduce the simultaneous emphasis in Summary/Skills. Keep the broad cloud list out of the headline framing and let the relevant experience bullets carry the evidence.

findings.issue: The most recent experience relies heavily on latency and percentage deltas, but does not provide a Tier 1 scope anchor such as corpus size, request volume, number of services, or team scale. That makes the infra ownership look thinner than the technical detail suggests.
findings.fix: Add one concrete scope metric to the lead TikTok bullet or project background, such as evaluation set size, number of services integrated, release cadence, or analyst/user volume.

revision_priority: [MUST] Reframe the cloud story so AWS/Azure/GCP are tied to the exact role or bullet where they were used, instead of being presented as one broad simultaneous core stack.
[MUST] Add one Tier 1 scale/scope anchor to the latest TikTok experience so the section does not read as pure outcome-percent polish.
[SHOULD] Remove bold from modifiers like internal/team-maintained/existing and reserve bold for technologies and quantified outcomes.
raw_json.revision_instructions: Keep the immutable role lines unchanged. Tighten the headline framing by splitting cloud evidence by role: TikTok can carry AWS, DiDi can carry Azure/GCP, and the Summary should stop reading like a broad multi-cloud bundle. Add one concrete Tier 1 scope metric to the TikTok lead bullet or its project background so the latest role has a real scale anchor, not just latency and percentage deltas. Clean up formatting by unbolding modifiers and leaving bold only for technologies, scope nouns, and quantified outcomes.

[SHOULD] Remove bold from modifiers like internal/team-maintained/existing and reserve bold for technologies and quantified outcomes.
